summary_markdown:
## What this issue is about
This issue focuses on enhancing the help message received when using the command `plonecli -l`. The goal is to provide clearer guidance for users, especially newcomers to the Plone CLI, when they attempt to create new add-ons.

## What needs to be done
The proposed enhancement is to improve the help output by including specific examples, particularly the command format for creating an add-on: `plonecli create addon src/collective.todo`. Before implementing this improvement, it's suggested to resolve issue #28, which may influence the current changes.

## Helpful background
Understanding the Plone CLI (Command Line Interface) tool is essential for interacting with Plone projects. The `-l` flag lists available templates, while the error related to missing arguments can be confusing for users who are not familiar with command-line operations.

## Good first steps
1. Review the current help output when using `plonecli -l`.
2. Familiarize yourself with the Plone CLI documentation, especially the section on creating add-ons: [Plone CLI Docs](https://github.com/plone/plonecli#creating-a-plone-add-on).
3. Check issue #28 for any existing discussions or updates that may affect this issue.
4. Consider drafting the improved help message based on your understanding.

Feel free to ask questions if anything is unclear!
